What has made AI such a big deal recently?
The goal of Artificial Intelligence (AI) is to give machines the ability to “think like humans” by simulating their cognitive processes. Although artificial intelligence (AI) will never be as smart as a human being, it already has many practical uses.
Many factors have converged to make the present an exciting period for significant AI progress.
- Over the past 60 years, computing power has increased by a factor of a trillion, making it far more efficient than it was back then.
- Data processing is becoming more cost-effective.
- Businesses are collecting more signals from client encounters, which means there is more data to study.
- The use of AI has already greatly enhanced consumer apps, raising hopes for further greater simplification and hence increasing demand for AI expertise and research.
How widespread is the use of artificial intelligence in our lives?
Absolutely to a greater extent. The vast majority of people who have access to a computer, smartphone, or other smart device are already making use of AI’s practical benefits:
- Personal voice-processing assistants like Siri and Cortana are available now.
- Image recognition is highly recommended for use in Facebook photo tagging.
- Amazon uses machine learning algorithms to provide product recommendations.
- Waze uses a mix of predictive algorithms, forecasting, and optimisation approaches to recommend the best routes for drivers to take.
How Can I Benefit from Artificial Intelligence?
The most basic kind of Artificial Intelligence will help with automating routine operations. Businesses may save a lot of time and money by using automation to handle data collecting, sorting, entering, and transformation. As it develops, it will be able to forecast your company’s future performance and tell you where you’re succeeding.
Do jobs in the field of Artificial Intelligence have a bright future?
In spite of “millions” of job openings, only roughly 300,000 people are working in artificial intelligence (AI). Programming expertise in Python, R, Java, and C++ are particularly in demand due to the widespread adoption of AI.
The World Economic Forum predicts that 97 million new employment will be produced by 2025 as a direct result of AI innovations, and this trend is expected to continue into the foreseeable future.
Who is eligible to enroll in this Artificial Intelligence Course in Chennai?
A wide variety of AI and Machine Learning tools and techniques are covered in our Artificial Intelligence training in Chennai. This course, developed by AI experts and aimed at developing professionals, is perfect for you if:
- Are a recent college graduate.
- Are contemplating a career change into artificial intelligence development.
- Have an interest in learning new skills to help you create, oversee, and organise AI-based solutions for your present company.
- Are looking for a way to continue their education at a higher level to improve their academic skills in this field.
What makes Softlogic Systems a good place to study?
At Softlogic Systems, you will receive hands-on experience and rigorous training from industry professionals. The course content covers both introductory and advanced-level material.
Learning from AI training in Chennai, which combines excellence and innovation, will provide you with abilities and expertise that are unique and essential in both your personal and professional lives.
When compared to competitors in the software training market, our placement services are unparalleled.
After completing the course, will I be awarded a certification?
After successfully completing the Artificial Training in Chennai, you will, indeed, be awarded a certificate of completion for your efforts. Our certification is an IBM-accredited one, which gives more credence to your CV and helps you stand out more prominently throughout the interview process.
How does Softlogics Placement team support us
The placement support offered by Softlogic increases your chances of getting the job of your dreams. Certified students who are interested in making a career change or entering the workforce for the first time will have access to comprehensive assistance through our placement assistance. Softlogic will provide the following premium services as part of our placement assistance:
- Resume building

Which organisations are actively looking to hire employees with expertise in AI?
Professionals that are skilled in artificial intelligence are in high demand across a wide range of organizations, including those of Google, Microsoft, Deloitte Accenture, IBM, Capgemini, Amazon, Apple, and many more MNCs.
What is the compensation package for AI professionals?
As there is a rising demand for AI specialists but a shortage of people with the necessary abilities, businesses are prepared to pay greater wages for trained AI professionals. The following table displays, according to Payscale, the annual salary that an AI engineer earns on average in key nations:
- India – INR 982K
- Canada – C$ 85,000

 the Artificial Intelligence Course
Future Scope of Learning AI Online Course
The science and engineering of teaching computers to perform tasks that require human intellect, like speech recognition, language translation, perception, and decision-making, is known as artificial intelligence. It is a branch of computer science that focuses on creating intelligent systems. AI careers include positions in data science, robotics, computer science, game programming, and intelligence, among others.
As a result, artificial intelligence is a highly sought-after course globally. To work in this sector, you should be able to speak at least one basic machine language.
AI Career Options Across Industries
Science and Research: The subject of drug discovery is growing quickly, and AI is greatly helping researchers in this regard. In biotechnology, researchers are now using AI to create bacteria for industrial uses.
Cybersecurity: To safeguard their resources and data, businesses are making significant investments in cybersecurity. The promise of AI in cybersecurity is bright. Cognitive AI is a strong representation of this field.
Data Analysis: Data analysis may benefit greatly from AI and machine learning. AI algorithms can get better through repetition, becoming more precise and accurate in the process.
Transport: Autonomous vehicles present a rather big possibility for AI in the future. A lot of companies are working on creating self-driving cars, which will mostly depend on AI and machine learning to work.
Home Assistance: People’s houses now have a dedicated area for AI. Popular smart home appliances like Google Home and Amazon Echo enable you to do many tasks with just your voice. Reshape your career through our Artificial Intelligence Online Course at SLA.
How does artificial intelligence work?
Cognitive skills such as the following are the focus of AI programming:
Learning: Creating rules to transform data into useful knowledge is the main objective of this area of AI programming. These rules—also referred to as algorithms—give computing systems detailed instructions on how to carry out particular tasks.
Reasoning: Finding the best algorithm to get a particular result is the main goal of this area of AI programming.
Self-rectification: Algorithms are continuously refined in this part of AI programming to guarantee the highest level of accuracy.
Creativity: To create new text, images, music, and ideas, this branch of AI employs neural networks, rules-based systems, statistical techniques, and other AI tools.

What has made AI such a big deal recently?
The goal of Artificial Intelligence (AI) is to give machines the ability to “think like humans” by simulating their cognitive processes. Although artificial intelligence (AI) will never be as smart as a human being, it already has many practical uses.
Many factors have converged to make the present an exciting period for significant AI progress.

The science and engineering of teaching computers to perform tasks that require human intellect, like speech recognition, language translation, perception, and decision-making, is known as artificial intelligence. How does artificial intelligence work?
Cognitive skills such as the following are the focus of AI programming:
Learning: Creating rules to transform data into useful knowledge is the main objective of this area of AI programming. These rules—also referred to as algorithms—give computing systems detailed instructions on how to carry out particular tasks.
Reasoning: Finding the best algorithm to get a particular result is the main goal of this area of AI programming.
Self-rectification: Algorithms are continuously refined in this part of AI programming to guarantee the highest level of accuracy.
Creativity: To create new text, images, music, and ideas, this branch of AI employs neural networks, rules-based systems, statistical techniques, and other AI tools.

Best For: Intermediate programmers (comfortable with Python). Now that we have completed the best 10 AI certification courses, let’s look into other aspects of your journey, which can help you decide on the courses, programs, or your career path better.
How to Choose the Right AI Course in India?
We believe that with so many AI programs available, choosing the right one depends on your career goals, learning style, and budget. Here are a few ways to evaluate which course will serve you best:
Based on Your Career Stage
- Students: If you’re still in college or just starting out, beginner-friendly or short-term certification courses are a good way to build a foundation before committing to advanced AI/ML programs.
- Working Professionals: Mid-career learners should look for structured, mentor-led courses that combine depth with career support, since placements, referrals, and project portfolios will make the transition easier.
- Domain Experts: If you already work in areas like healthcare, finance, or manufacturing, a specialization in AI applications within your field (e.g., computer vision for healthcare, NLP for finance) can add the most value.
Scaler Placement Report and Statistics
Scaler learners achieved 2.5x salary growth with average post-Scaler CTC reaching ₹23L.
Based on the Learning Format
- Online: Flexible and self-paced, perfect for busy schedules and learners who want to balance study with work.
- Offline: Great for those who prefer classroom interaction, but options are limited and often costlier.
- Hybrid: A mix of online lectures and occasional offline sessions can be ideal for those who want flexibility with some face-to-face engagement.
Based on Specialization
- Core Machine Learning: Best for building the foundation needed to understand algorithms and models.
- Deep Learning: Suited for those who want to explore neural networks, computer vision, or generative AI.
- Natural Language Processing (NLP): Ideal if you’re interested in chatbots, language models, or text analytics.
- Computer Vision: The right choice for those drawn to image recognition, autonomous vehicles, or medical imaging applications.
Based on Duration and Fee
- Short-term vs Long-term: Short-term courses (a few weeks to months) are good for upskilling quickly, while long-term programs (12-18 months) offer depth, alumni status, and stronger career outcomes.
- Affordable vs Premium: Free or budget-friendly courses (like Fast.ai or Coursera basics) are great starting points. Premium programs from IITs, Stanford, or Scaler come at a higher fee but offer structured mentorship, projects, and strong brand value, which makes them worthwhile for career growth.
Must-Have Skills Before Taking an AI Course
Before enrolling in an AI course in India, it’s helpful to know what skills will make your learning journey smoother. You don’t need to be an expert, but having the right foundation ensures you can keep up with the pace of most programs.
Technical Skills
A basic understanding of Python programming is almost essential, since it’s the language most AI frameworks use. Practice with maths concepts like linear algebra, probability, and statistics will help you understand how algorithms work under the hood. Even if your math is rusty, brushing up on these fundamentals can go a long way in building confidence.
Soft Skills
For your career, soft skills are as important as having technical skills. Strong logical thinking, curiosity, and problem-solving skills will help you apply technical knowledge to your day-to-day tasks. Since the field evolves rapidly, a willingness to keep learning and experimenting is just as important as technical know-how.
AI Certification Value in the Indian Job Market
Recruiters often see a credible AI certification as proof that you’ve invested time in structured learning and gained exposure to industry-relevant tools. While a certificate alone won’t guarantee a job, pairing it with projects and a visible portfolio can significantly boost your profile.
This is what we advocate at Scaler. We had seen how many individuals lacked the industry-required skills even after getting a degree, or most of them had a hard time upskilling to match industry standards. Hence, we have built our programs in a way that a learner gets the resources from all sides, i.e, from detailed lectures, to mentorship, till acquiring job-readiness. We walk alongside our learners through their journey.
Check out: [Scaler’s AI and ML Program](https://www.scaler.com/ai-machine-learning-course/)
Conclusion: Which AI Course in India is Right for You?
The right AI course in India depends on where you are in your journey and what you want to achieve. If you’re a student or beginner, short and beginner-friendly programs on Coursera or Fast.ai can help you explore AI concepts at little to no cost. If you’re a working professional aiming to switch careers or climb into higher-paying roles, structured and mentor-led programs like those from Scaler or IITs provide the depth, credibility, and career support you’ll need. And if you’re already an expert, advanced options from MIT, Stanford, or NVIDIA can help you specialize further.
What matters most is taking the first step. AI is reshaping industries in India at a rapid pace, and waiting too long can mean missing out on opportunities. Choose the program that matches your career goals, commit to building projects, and keep learning.
FAQs
Which is the best AI course in India for freshers?
For beginners, short and beginner-friendly YouTube videos or Coursera’s courses can be a good option. If you want a structured, career-focused path from the start, Scaler’s AI & ML course is among the best for freshers aiming to move into AI roles.
Can I do an AI course without coding experience?
Yes, you can. Many courses are designed for learners from a non-CS background. However, if your goal is to build AI applications, you’ll eventually need basic Python and math skills, which most paid programs, including Scaler, teach from scratch.
What is the average salary after an AI course in India?
Salaries depend on your experience and role. Freshers with AI skills usually earn ₹5-8 LPA, while mid-level professionals earn ₹12-18 LPA. Senior AI engineers or researchers can cross ₹25 LPA, especially in product companies and MNCs.
Are IIT-certified AI courses better for career growth?
Yes, IIT-certified programs often carry strong brand recognition and credibility in the Indian job market. They’re especially helpful for career switchers and mid-level professionals. But equally important is the hands-on exposure you get from mentor-led programs like Scaler, which balance academic depth with career outcomes.
Can I do an AI course part-time while working?
Yes. Most online and hybrid AI courses are designed to be flexible. If you’re a working professional, look for programs that offer self-paced modules or weekend live classes. Scaler’s live-online model, for example, is built to fit around full-time jobs.
